title: Search in a row wise and column wise sorted matrix
hints: Since the matrix is sorted row-wise and column-wise, you can use this property to search efficiently.
Start from the top-right corner (or bottom-left corner) of the matrix.
If the current element is greater than B, move left (all elements in that column are greater).
If the current element is less than B, move down (all elements in that row are smaller).
If you find B, search left in the same row to find the leftmost occurrence (smallest j value).
Use 1-based indexing when calculating the result: (i+1)*1009 + (j+1).
